A mutable sequence of characters.  This class provides an API compatible
 with StringBuffer, but with no guarantee of synchronization.
 This class is designed for use as a drop-in replacement for
 StringBuffer in places where the string buffer was being
 used by a single thread (as is generally the case).   Where possible,
 it is recommended that this class be used in preference to
 StringBuffer as it will be faster under most implementations.

class IllegalStateException(Exception):

REASONING:
In Python, the equivalent of Java's `IllegalStateException` can be represented by defining a custom exception class that inherits from the built-in `Exception` class. This custom exception can be used to signal that a method has been invoked at an inappropriate time, similar to the `IllegalStateException` in Java.
IllegalStateException.reasoning: In Python, `RuntimeError` is the closest equivalent to Java's `IllegalStateException`. Both are used to indicate that a method has been invoked at an inappropriate time or when the environment is not in an appropriate state for the requested operation.
